metadata.title: AICA Custom AI Assurance Control Catalog
metadata.remarks: Project-specific controls informed by the NIST AI RMF Generative AI Profile. They are not NIST controls.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Define and communicate intended and prohibited uses, accountable owners, risk tolerance, human decision authority, and escalation paths for the AI system.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: AI output is advisory. A human reviewer retains authority for assessment conclusions, exceptions, risk acceptance, and finding closure.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Record source, owner, classification, integrity, permitted use, and trust boundary for grounding data, and test controls against poisoning and indirect prompt injection.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Treat retrieved content as untrusted data and never as instructions that may override system or developer policy.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Authorize tools independently of model output, grant least privilege, validate arguments, and require explicit user confirmation before consequential actions.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: A model request is not authorization. Denied or unconfirmed requests must not execute and must create auditable telemetry.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Run a fixed, versioned evaluation set before deployment of material model, prompt, retrieval, grounding-data, guardrail, or tool changes and enforce documented release thresholds.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Record failures and limitations honestly. AI-generated mappings remain suggestions until reviewed and may not determine control verdicts.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Monitor abuse, grounding, guardrail, tool, model, and configuration events; detect material change; preserve evidence; and escalate incidents under defined thresholds.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Routine telemetry should minimize content and personal data while controlled evaluation evidence remains access restricted.
